---
title: "Zak Hynd"
---

Geospatial data scientist working across remote sensing, geostatistics, and spatial Python. Behind that sits a decade building geoscience software as a product person, and a Master's in Geospatial Data Science bringing the technical depth forward.

The product decade was systems design. The work was taking competing demands from different business units and turning them into something a development team could build against: a feature-priority framework for a geomodelling platform used across industries, a customer feedback loop run across most of the decade, a process for getting working prototypes in front of users before release. The same instinct runs through [how I work with AI](projects/ai-workflow.qmd) now – tasks divided deliberately, intermediate output checked, the model directed rather than deferred to.

The geology runs underneath all of it. Fieldwork, data wrangling, processing, structural interpretation – that domain knowledge sits beneath the SAR work now. [River braid mapping](projects/river-braid.qmd) is the clearest current case: Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical fused to track river-channel change after the 2021 Canterbury flood, built as a proof of concept for catchment-management workflows.

Before any of this, geology and zoology at Otago, and a touch of Iceland. Christchurch-based, open to remote.

[See all projects →](projects.qmd)
